name: Khamenei's Compound — Pasteur District, Tehran
detail: 30 bombs by Israeli jets at ~08:10 IRST. Khamenei + family killed. 48 leaders eliminated.

name: Thar-Allah HQ — Tehran
detail: IRGC command center for Tehran security. IDF released video of strike.

name: Revolutionary Court — Shariati St, Tehran
detail: Building completely destroyed.

name: IRIB Broadcasting HQ — Alvand St, Tehran
detail: State TV interrupted. Broadcasts hacked to air Trump and Netanyahu speeches.

name: Armed Forces General Staff — Sepah Square
detail: Extensive bombing of military command infrastructure.

name: Shajareh Tayyebeh School — Minab
detail: 148–180 children killed (contested). School was ~200ft from IRGC Navy base.

name: Natanz Nuclear Facility
detail: Ground-penetrating munitions. Already damaged in June 2025.

name: Fordow Underground Facility
detail: Access roads and entrance hit. Deep damage uncertain per IAEA.

name: Niloofar Square — Tehran
detail: 20 civilians killed on March 2.

name: Interior Ministry — Tehran
detail: Coordinated IRGC/Basij operations hub.

name: IRGC Navy HQ — Bandar Abbas
detail: Port facilities and fast-attack craft destroyed. Hormuz Strait patrol boats sunk.

name: Parchin Military Complex
detail: Suspected weapons research site. B-2 bunker busters deployed. IAEA denied access pre-strike.

name: Tabriz Air Base
detail: IRIAF fighter jets destroyed on tarmac. Runway cratered.

name: Missile Storage — Khorramabad
detail: Underground ballistic missile depot. Secondary explosions lasted 4 hours.

name: Chabahar Port Facilities
detail: Drone and missile assembly warehouse targeted. India condemns strike near joint project.

name: Isfahan University of Technology
detail: Alleged drone R&D lab. 12 civilians killed. Iran calls it war crime.

name: Azadi Sports Complex — Tehran
detail: 12,000-seat indoor arena destroyed by U.S.-Israeli airstrike.

name: Supreme National Security Council HQ — Tehran
detail: Building destroyed; several senior officials killed.

name: Expediency Discernment Council — Tehran
detail: Facility targeted and destroyed.

name: Bushehr Airport
detail: Terminal damaged; Iran Air Airbus A319 destroyed.

name: Min Zadai Underground Facility
detail: Suspected nuclear weapons site destroyed.

name: Tehran and Kermanshah Strikes
detail: Israeli airstrikes targeted Tehran and Kermanshah, disabling much of Iran’s air defenses and missile infrastructure.

name: Beirut Strikes
detail: Israeli airstrikes targeted southern suburbs of Beirut following a mass evacuation order.

name: Iranian Drone Carrier Strike
detail: U.S. forces struck the Iranian drone carrier IRIS Shahid Bagheri at sea.

name: Tehran Air Defense Systems
detail: Israeli airstrikes targeted and significantly damaged Iran's air defense and missile infrastructure in Tehran.

name: Beirut's Southern Suburbs
detail: Israeli airstrikes hit Hezbollah-held areas in Beirut following a mass evacuation order.

name: Iranian Drone Carrier
detail: U.S. forces struck and destroyed an Iranian drone carrier at sea.

name: Iranian Missile Facilities
detail: U.S. B-2 bombers destroyed deeply buried missile launch facilities and other key military sites in Iran.

name: Iranian Naval Vessels
detail: U.S. forces sank over 40 Iranian warships, significantly degrading Iran's naval capabilities.

name: Tehran's Mehrabad International Airport
detail: U.S.-Israeli airstrikes targeted the airport, causing significant damage to infrastructure.

name: Halliburton Oil Facilities — Basra, Iraq
detail: Iranian missile strikes set U.S.-based Halliburton and KBR facilities ablaze.

name: Iranian Naval Vessels
detail: U.S. forces sank over 30 Iranian ships, including a drone carrier set ablaze.

name: Iranian Ballistic Missile Launchers
detail: U.S. B-2 bombers destroyed deeply buried missile launch facilities, reducing Iranian missile launches by 90%.

name: Hezbollah Command Centers — Beirut
detail: Israeli airstrikes targeted multiple Hezbollah command centers in Beirut.

name: Tripoli — Northern Lebanon
detail: Israeli airstrikes hit the city of Tripoli for the first time in the conflict.

name: Iranian Air Defense Systems
detail: Israeli military claimed near-complete air superiority after destroying 80% of Iranian air defense systems.

name: Oil Storage Facility — Tehran
detail: Israeli airstrikes targeted a vital oil storage facility in Tehran, marking a significant escalation as civil infrastructure becomes a target for the first time.

name: Mehrabad International Airport — Tehran
detail: Israeli airstrikes targeted Tehran's Mehrabad International Airport, reportedly used for arms transport.

name: Isfahan Military Targets
detail: U.S. and Israeli forces executed strikes on multiple targets in Isfahan, focusing on military infrastructure.

name: Tehran Fuel Depots
detail: Israeli airstrikes targeted 30 fuel depots in Tehran, causing massive fires and toxic smoke over the city.

name: Isfahan Airport
detail: Israeli airstrikes targeted Iranian F-14 fighter jets at Isfahan Airport.

name: Iranian Oil Facilities
detail: Joint U.S.-Israeli airstrikes targeted oil facilities in Tehran and other cities, causing large fires and civilian casualties.

name: Aghdasieh Oil Warehouse & Shahran Depot — Tehran
detail: Israeli strikes on March 10 targeted the Aghdasieh oil warehouse (northeast Tehran), Shahran oil depot (north Tehran), a south Tehran refinery, and a Karaj depot. At least 40 killed. Toxic black smoke engulfed Tehran; residents reported oil-tainted rainfall. IDF stated the facilities distributed fuel to IRGC military entities.

name: Yehud — Cluster Warhead Strike, Central Israel
detail: Iran fired a missile with a cluster warhead that struck multiple sites in Yehud, a city east of Tel Aviv, killing 2 civilians and wounding dozens. One of the most destructive single Iranian strikes on Israeli civilians since the war began.

name: Strait of Hormuz — 16 Iranian Mine-Laying Vessels Destroyed
detail: After US intelligence confirmed Iran had begun laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, CENTCOM destroyed 16 mine-laying vessels and mine storage facilities. Gen. Caine stated US forces are 'hunting and striking mine-laying vessels.' Trump announced the destruction of 10 'inactive mine-laying boats and/or ships' on Truth Social and warned of 'death, fire and fury' if mines are not removed. Iran's navy is now effectively eliminated — 43 vessels total destroyed or damaged.

name: Haifa Oil & Gas Refinery — Israel
detail: The Iranian army launched a drone attack targeting an Israeli oil and gas refinery and fuel tanks in Haifa, according to Tasnim news agency. The Israeli army confirmed it identified incoming launches from Iran and worked to intercept. Marks the first confirmed targeting of Israeli energy infrastructure in Haifa.

name: Pentagon Investigation — Minab Girls' School Strike
detail: The Pentagon formally opened an investigation into the US missile strike on the Shajareh Tayyebeh Elementary School in Minab that killed at least 165 civilians, many of them children. A preliminary internal assessment suggested the US was at fault. The school was located ~200ft from an IRGC Navy base. The formal investigation was announced March 12.

name: Israeli Strikes — Hezbollah Command, Beirut (Mar 12)
detail: Israel launched airstrikes against Hezbollah launch sites and command infrastructure in response to Hezbollah's biggest rocket barrage since the war began (joint IRGC-Hezbollah operation striking 50+ targets in 5 hours). Separately, an Israeli strike in Beirut killed 4 Iranian diplomats, prompting Iran to call the act 'terrorism' at the UN Security Council.

name: US-Israel — 6,000+ Targets Struck Across Iran (CENTCOM Total)
detail: CENTCOM confirmed approximately 6,000 targets have been struck across Iran's 30/31 provinces since operations began. 90+ Iranian naval vessels damaged/destroyed. 92% reduction in Iranian ballistic missile launch rate from peak. 80% of Iranian air defense degraded. 30+ mine-laying ships destroyed. Trump: 'There is practically nothing left to hit.'

name: KC-135 Crash — Western Iraq (4th US Aircraft Lost)
detail: US Air Force KC-135 Stratotanker crashed in western Iraq supporting Epic Fury ops. CENTCOM: 'not due to hostile or friendly fire.' Islamic Resistance in Iraq claims shootdown. 6 crew onboard, no ejection seats. Second tanker (also KC-135) landed safely in Israel. Fourth US aircraft lost in the conflict.
